description: Letter from Yaʿaqov b. Yeshaʿya ha-Kohen to Yiṣḥaq Ḥemdat ha-Yeshiva. The addressee might be Abū Isḥāq Avraham b. Natan ha-Sheviʿi who was also known as Ḥemdat ha-Yeshiva. Very faded. Recto is entirely in Hebrew and mentions Yeshuʿa ha-Levi and a certain Ṣedaqa. Also mentions danger on the roads (l. 20). Verso switches to Judaeo-Arabic. The sender states that he heard that the addressee is in possession of a document from the caliph (kitāb min amīr al-muʾminīn). He asks for a favor from the addressee, Ḥemdat ha-Yeshiva, including sending something with the same Yeshuʿa ha-Levi mentioned on recto. More of the text may be legible with multispectral imaging. (Information in part from Goitein's index cards)
description_he: מכתב בHebrew, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
